metadata.subject: English Language Arts
metadata.description: New Media 10 explores digital and multimodal communication in the 21st century. Students analyze and create texts using digital tools — from websites and social media to video, podcasts, and interactive media. The course develops digital citizenship, critical media literacy, and the ability to communicate effectively through new media forms. Students examine how digital environments shape language, identity, and relationships.
metadata.general_description: A study of digital and multimodal communication, developing digital citizenship, critical media literacy, and the ability to create and analyze texts using contemporary digital forms and tools.

big_ideas.statement: The exploration of text and story deepens our understanding of diverse, complex ideas about identity, others, and the world.
big_ideas.elaboration: Digital and new media storytelling are powerful forms of expression — from YouTube documentaries to Instagram narratives to interactive games.

big_ideas.statement: People understand text differently depending on their world views and perspectives.
big_ideas.elaboration: Digital media is consumed through the filter of personal identity and experience — and algorithms can reinforce existing perspectives rather than expanding them.

big_ideas.statement: Texts are socially, culturally, geographically, and historically constructed.
big_ideas.elaboration: Digital texts — like all texts — are made in specific contexts and reflect specific values, biases, and purposes. Understanding this is core to digital literacy.

big_ideas.statement: Language shapes ideas and influences others.
big_ideas.elaboration: In new media environments, language — including hashtags, memes, captions, and visual rhetoric — shapes public opinion and cultural narratives at a massive scale.

big_ideas.statement: Digital citizens take personal responsibility and behave ethically and cautiously when using technology.
big_ideas.elaboration: Being a digital citizen means understanding that online actions have real consequences — for privacy, relationships, communities, and democracy.

big_ideas.statement: Digital citizens have rights and responsibilities in an increasingly globalized society.
big_ideas.elaboration: In a networked world, individuals have the right to access information and the responsibility to use digital platforms ethically, inclusively, and thoughtfully.

categories.name: Comprehend and Connect (Reading, Listening, Viewing)
categories.raw: Comprehend and Connect — Reading, Listening, Viewing

competencies.raw: Recognize the complexities of digital citizenship; read for enjoyment and personal goals.
i_can_statements: I can explain what digital citizenship means and give examples of responsible digital behaviour.
I can identify ethical and unethical uses of digital platforms.
I can navigate digital media for personal learning and enjoyment with awareness of its risks and benefits.

competencies.raw: Explore story and narrative in expressing First Peoples perspectives in new media; explore diversity among First Peoples cultures in new media.
i_can_statements: I can find and engage with First Peoples content in digital media — film, social media, podcasts, websites.
I can evaluate digital representations of First Peoples for authenticity and cultural sensitivity.
I can explain how digital platforms offer new possibilities for Indigenous storytelling and self-representation.

competencies.raw: Access information from diverse sources; apply comprehension strategies to multimodal texts; identify bias, contradictions, and distortions.
i_can_statements: I can evaluate the credibility and reliability of digital sources using tools and criteria.
I can identify misinformation, disinformation, and propaganda in digital media.
I can analyze multimodal texts (combining text, image, sound, and interactivity) for meaning and purpose.
I can recognize filter bubbles and algorithmic bias in digital information environments.

competencies.raw: Recognize how forms and features enhance meaning; explore how language constructs identities.
i_can_statements: I can analyze how design choices (layout, colour, font, image) in a digital text shape its meaning.
I can explain how digital platforms and social media influence identity construction.
I can analyze how memes, hashtags, and viral content function as cultural language.

categories.name: Create and Communicate (Writing, Speaking, Representing)
categories.raw: Create and Communicate — Writing, Speaking, Representing

competencies.raw: Respectfully exchange ideas and viewpoints from diverse perspectives; use digital media to collaborate and communicate.
i_can_statements: I can collaborate with others using digital tools, platforms, and shared documents.
I can communicate and engage with others online respectfully and ethically.
I can use digital platforms to share and exchange ideas with authentic audiences.

competencies.raw: Respond to text in personal, creative, and critical ways; assess and refine texts to improve clarity and impact.
i_can_statements: I can create a digital response to a text in an appropriate new media form.
I can critically analyze a digital media product and explain how it constructs meaning.
I can revise and refine a digital media project to improve clarity, impact, and accessibility.

competencies.raw: Use writing and design processes for varied audiences and purposes; use conventions and citations; transform ideas to create original texts.
i_can_statements: I can create original digital media projects (video, podcast, website, infographic) for real audiences.
I can make intentional design decisions and explain how they serve my purpose and audience.
I can properly attribute sources and respect copyright in my digital work.
I can apply appropriate conventions of spelling, grammar, and style in digital writing.

areas.name: Digital Text Forms and Genres
areas.raw: Literary or thematic categories; interactivity and multimodal text features; narrative structures in First Peoples texts.
topics: Social media: posts, stories, reels, threads, and their rhetorical functions
Websites and blogs: purpose, audience, and design
Video: documentary, vlog, short film, explainer, advertisement
Podcasts and audio: storytelling, journalism, and commentary
Infographics and data visualization
Interactive media: games, simulations, hypertext fiction
Memes, GIFs, and viral content as cultural communication
First Peoples digital storytelling and online self-representation

areas.name: Text Features and Structures (Digital)
areas.raw: Interactivity and multimodal text features; narrative structures in First Peoples texts; protocols related to ownership.
topics: Multimodal text analysis: combining text, image, audio, and interactivity
Digital design principles: layout, colour, font, white space, hierarchy
Hypertext and non-linear navigation
Algorithms and feed curation: how they shape what we see
Copyright, Creative Commons, and intellectual property in digital contexts
First Peoples protocols for digital use of cultural material

areas.name: Strategies and Processes
areas.raw: Reading, oral language, and metacognitive strategies; new media design processes; multimedia presentation processes; writing processes.
topics: Digital research and source evaluation: SIFT method (Stop, Investigate, Find, Trace)
Lateral reading: checking sources across multiple sites
New media design process: research, prototype, test, revise, publish
Storyboarding for video and audio projects
Digital writing process: drafting, revising, editing for web and social media

areas.name: Digital Citizenship and Language
areas.raw: Elements of style, usage, and conventions; literary techniques and devices; citation techniques.
topics: Digital citizenship: privacy, security, digital footprint, online identity
Ethical use of AI-generated content
Online safety: cyberbullying, phishing, data privacy
Language in digital spaces: register, tone, abbreviation, emoji as communication
Citation in digital contexts: linking, crediting, and acknowledging sources
Accessibility in digital communication: alt text, captions, readability
